title: P(B_1 > 0 and B_2 < 0)

source.file: content/A Practical Guide To Quantitative Finance Interviews Book by Xinfeng Zhou (chapters)/chapter-05-stochastic-processes.md

setup.narrative: B_t is a standard Brownian motion. Consider the event {B_1 > 0 AND B_2 < 0}.
rules: B_2 = B_1 + (B_2 - B_1) where B_2 - B_1 is independent of B_1, also ~ N(0, 1).
setup.question: What is P(B_1 > 0 and B_2 < 0)?

learningObjectives: Decompose B_2 = B_1 + (B_2 - B_1) into independent N(0, 1) parts X and Y.
Translate the joint condition into a 2D region.
Use symmetry to recognize 8 equally-likely sectors and pick the one matching the event.

stages.label: Decompose B_2
stages.prompt: Let X = B_1 and Y = B_2 - B_1. Then X, Y are independent N(0, 1). Express B_2 in terms of X, Y.
stages.explanation: B_2 = B_1 + (B_2 - B_1) = X + Y. The event {B_2 < 0} means X + Y < 0, equivalently Y < -X.

stages.label: Geometry of the favorable region
stages.prompt: We want P(X > 0 AND Y < -X). In the (X, Y) plane this is the region with X > 0 (right half) AND Y < -X (below the line Y = -X). What does this look like?

options.text: The 'bottom-right' triangle bounded by the positive X-axis (above), the line Y = -X with negative slope (above), and infinity to the right and below.

stages.explanation: X > 0 is the right half. Y < -X means below the line through the origin with slope -1. The intersection is a wedge in the lower-right region.

stages.label: Use 8-fold symmetry
stages.prompt: The joint density of (X, Y) is rotationally symmetric around the origin (radial). The four lines x = 0, y = 0, y = x, y = -x divide the plane into 8 equal-probability sectors. Which sector(s) match X > 0 AND Y < -X?

options.text: Exactly ONE of the 8 sectors. So P = 1/8.

stages.explanation: The 8 sectors are bounded by x = 0, y = 0, y = x, y = -x. The condition (X > 0, Y < -X) defines the sector below the line y = -x and to the right of x = 0 -- one of the 8.

stages.label: Commit
stages.explanation: 1/8. The sign-pattern probability arises directly from the 8-fold rotational symmetry of two independent N(0, 1).

stages.label: Reflection
prompts: Compute P(B_1 < 0 AND B_2 > 0). Same answer by symmetry.
Compute P(B_1 and B_2 have same sign). Equals 1 - 2 * (1/8) - 2 * (1/8) = ... actually compute carefully.
Generalize: P(B_1 > 0 AND B_2 < 0 AND B_3 > 0)? Use the 4-D version of the geometric argument.
